Paragraph.Text: is a director or an officer of, or has a material interest in, any person who is or is likely to be materially affected by any action taken or proposed to be taken by the Bank or the Governor under the Payment Clearing and Settlement Act.

Subsection.Text: The disclosure shall be made as soon as the director becomes aware of the contract, transaction or action.
A director who is required to make a disclosure shall not vote on any resolution to approve the contract, transaction or action, unless it relates to directors’ fees.

For the purpose of this section, a general notice to the Board by a director, declaring that he or she is a director or officer of or has a material interest in a person, and that he or she is to be regarded as interested in a contract or transaction entered into with that person or an action that affects that person, is a sufficient declaration of interest in relation to a contract or transaction with that person or action that affects that person.

Section.Text: Section 12 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Governor is Chair of the Board of Directors.
Section 16 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Before a person starts to act as a director, an officer or an employee of the Bank, he or she shall take an oath, or make a solemn affirmation, of fidelity and secrecy, in the form set out in the schedule, before a commissioner for taking affidavits.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 18(d) and (e) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: buy and sell securities issued or guaranteed by the Government of the United States of America or Japan or the government of a country in the European Union;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 18(g.1) to (i) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (g.1)
Paragraph.Text: if the Governor is of the opinion that there is a severe and unusual stress on a financial market or financial system, buy and sell any other securities, treasury bills, obligations, bills of exchange or promissory notes, to the extent determined necessary by the Governor for the purpose of promoting the stability of the Canadian financial system;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: make loans or advances for periods not exceeding six months to members of the Canadian Payments Association on taking security in any property that the institution to which the loan or advance is made is authorized to hold;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: make loans or advances for periods not exceeding six months to the Government of Canada or the government of a province on taking security in readily marketable securities issued or guaranteed by Canada or any province;

Section.Text: Sections 19 and 20 of the Act are replaced by the following:

If the Bank takes any action under paragraph 18(g.1), the Bank shall cause a notice to be published in the Canada Gazette that the Governor has formed an opinion that there is a severe and unusual stress on a financial market or financial system. The notice is to be published as soon as the Governor is of the opinion that its publication will not materially contribute to the stress to which the notice relates.
The Bank may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: acquire from any bank or authorized foreign bank that is not subject to the restrictions and requirements referred to in subsection 524(2) of the Bank Act and hold any property held by the bank or authorized foreign bank as security under Part VIII of the Bank Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: exercise every right and remedy in respect of any security acquired under paragraph (a) that could have been exercised by the bank or authorized foreign bank.

Section.Text: The definition federal financial institution in subsection 22(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: federal financial institution means a bank, an authorized foreign bank, a company to which the Trust and Loan Companies Act applies or an association to which the Cooperative Credit Associations Act applies;

Section.Text: Subsection 24(3) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Canadian Government cheques to be paid or negotiated at par
Subsection.Text: The Bank shall not make any charge for cashing or negotiating a cheque drawn on the Receiver General or on the account of the Receiver General, or for cashing or negotiating any other instrument issued as authority for the payment of money out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, or on a cheque drawn in favour of the Government of Canada or any of its departments and tendered for deposit in the Consolidated Revenue Fund.
Subsections 25(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:
The Bank has the sole right to issue notes and those notes shall be a first charge on the assets of the Bank.
It is the duty of the Bank to make adequate arrangements for the issue of its notes in Canada and to supply those notes as required for circulation in Canada.
Subsection 25(3) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:
Les coupures des billets de la Banque, de même que leurs modalités d’impression et de validation, sont déterminées par règlement du gouverneur en conseil.
Subsections 25(4) and (5) of the Act are replaced by the following:
The form and material of the notes of the Bank shall be subject to approval by the Minister, but each note shall be printed in both the English and French languages.
Notwithstanding anything contained in this section, each note of the Bank printed before June 23, 1936, whether issued before, on or after that date, is a valid and binding obligation of the Bank.

Section.Text: Section 29 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Bank shall, as soon as practicable after the close of business on Wednesday of each week, make up and transmit to the Minister its balance sheet as at the close of business on that day.

The Bank shall, as soon as practicable after the last business day of each month, make up and transmit to the Minister its balance sheet as at the close of business on that day. The balance sheet shall set out information regarding the Bank’s investments in securities issued or guaranteed by the Government of Canada.
A copy of each balance sheet shall be published in the issue of the Canada Gazette next following its transmission to the Minister.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 30:

Section.MarginalNote: No liability if in good faith
Section.Text: No action lies against Her Majesty, the Minister, any officer, employee or director of the Bank or any person acting under the direction of the Governor for anything done or omitted to be done in good faith in the administration or discharge of any powers or duties that under this Act are intended or authorized to be executed or performed.
Sections 31 to 33 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Every person who holds office or continues to hold office as the Governor or as a Deputy Governor or director of the Bank, knowing that he or she is not eligible for that office, is guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a fine of not more than one hundred thousand dollars or to imprisonment for a term of not more than six months or to both.

Section.MarginalNote: Verifying false statement, account or list
Section.Text: Every director, officer or auditor of the Bank who verifies any statement, account or list required to be furnished to the Minister pursuant to this Act, or who has to do with the delivering or transmitting of that statement, account or list to the Minister, knowing it to be false in any material particular, is guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a fine of not more than one hundred thousand dollars or to imprisonment for a term of not more than six months or to both.

Any officer of the Bank or any officer of a bank or any other person who fails or omits to comply with any provision of this Act is guilty of an offence and, unless otherwise provided by this Act, liable on summary conviction to a fine of not more than one hundred thousand dollars or to imprisonment for a term of not more than six months or to both.
Schedules I to III to the Act are replaced by the schedule set out in Schedule 3 to this Act.
The definitions federal institution and member institution in section 2 of the Canada Deposit Insurance Corporation Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: federal institution means a bank, company or association referred to in section 8;
member institution means a corporation that has deposit insurance under this Act;

Section.Text: Paragraphs 5(1)(b) to (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the persons who for the time being hold the offices of the Governor of the Bank of Canada, the Deputy Minister of Finance, the Superintendent of Financial Institutions and the Commissioner of the Financial Consumer Agency of Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: a Deputy Superintendent of Financial Institutions, or an officer of the Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions, appointed by the Minister; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: not more than five other members appointed by the Minister with the approval of the Governor in Council.

Section.Text: Section 8 of the Act is amended by striking out the word “and” at the end of paragraph (a), by adding the word “and” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an association to which the Cooperative Credit Associations Act applies.

Section.Text: Subsection 10(1) of the Act is amended by striking out the word “and” at the end of paragraph (i) and by adding the following after paragraph (i):

Paragraph.Label: (i.1)
Paragraph.Text: settle or compromise any claim by or against the Corporation; and

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 23(1) of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The premium payable by a member institution in respect of the premium year in which it becomes a member institution shall be the same proportion of the lesser of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the annual premium for that member determined by by-law made under subsection 21(2) based on an amount equal to the sum of so much of the deposits as are considered to be insured by the Corporation and deposited with the member institution as of the end of the month in which it becomes a member institution, and

Section.Text: Section 24.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

No member institution shall, without the prior agreement of the Corporation, reduce or extinguish a premium payment, interest or other payment to be made to the Corporation by reason of a set-off or claim by the member institution against the Corporation.
Paragraph 26.03(1)(a) of the Act is repealed.
Section 29 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The person who conducts an examination under section 27 or an inspection under section 28 in respect of a member institution shall make all examinations or inspections that the person considers necessary to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: provide, by way of a rating or any other means, an assessment of the safety and soundness of the member institution, including its financial condition;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: comment on the operations of the member institution, taking into account the standards of sound business and financial practices established by the by-laws; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the member institution is a provincial institution and the Corporation and the person agree, comment on whether the provisions of the statutes governing the provincial institution are being complied with.

Subsection.Text: The person shall provide written reports to the Corporation on the matters referred to in paragraphs (1)(a) to (c) in a timely manner.

The Corporation is entitled to all information obtained by or produced by or for the person, whether in the course of conducting an examination or inspection or otherwise, regarding the affairs of the member institution or any of its affiliates or subsidiaries or of any person dealing with the member institution or any of its affiliates or subsidiaries, that relates to the safety and soundness, or the operations, of the member institution.

Without limiting subsection (3), the person shall provide the Corporation with any information that the person considers relevant to any matter referred to in any of paragraphs (1)(a) to (c) or to any report provided under subsection (2).

The person shall without delay inform the Corporation if, at any time, whether in the course of conducting an examination or inspection or otherwise, there comes to the attention of the person any change in the circumstances of the member institution that might materially affect the position of the Corporation as an insurer.

Section.Text: If requested to do so by the Corporation, the person who conducts an examination under section 27 or an inspection under section 28 in respect of a member institution shall review, or cause another person to review on the person’s behalf, within the time specified by the Corporation, the correctness of the returns made by the member institution on which its premiums are based and through which its premium classification is in part determined.

Section.MarginalNote: Certain reports to be provided to Corporation
Section.Text: When a report is sent by the Superintendent to the Minister under section 643 of the Bank Act, section 505 of the Trust and Loan Companies Act or section 437 of the Cooperative Credit Associations Act, a copy of the report shall be sent by the Superintendent at the same time to the Corporation.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 39.1(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the viability of the federal member institution cannot be restored or preserved by the exercise of the Superintendent’s powers under the Bank Act, the Trust and Loan Companies Act or the Cooperative Credit Associations Act,

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 39.1(2)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the federal member institution’s regulatory capital, within the meaning assigned to that expression by the Bank Act, the Trust and Loan Companies Act or the Cooperative Credit Associations Act, whichever is applicable, is or is about to become substantially deficient; or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 39.1(3)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: circumstances exist in respect of a federal member institution that would allow the Superintendent to take control of the federal member institution under the Bank Act, the Trust and Loan Companies Act or the Cooperative Credit Associations Act, and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 39.15(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsections (1) and (2) do not apply so as to prevent a member of the Canadian Payments Association from acting or ceasing to act as a clearing agent for a federal member institution in accordance with the Canadian Payments Act and the by-laws and rules of that Association.
Paragraph 39.15(6)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Superintendent, on the application of the federal member institution, exempted the security agreement from the application of those paragraphs and that subsection before the making of an order under subsection 39.13(1).

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 39.19(1)(a) to (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: sections 407, 407.01, 407.02, 407.03, 407.1, 407.2, 408, 411, 428 and 430 of the Insurance Companies Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: sections 375, 375.1, 376, 379 and 396 and subsection 399(2) of the Trust and Loan Companies Act.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 39.19(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

An exemption from the application of section 385 of the Bank Act, section 411 of the Insurance Companies Act or section 379 of the Trust and Loan Companies Act that is granted under section 388 of the Bank Act, section 414 of the Insurance Companies Act or section 382 of the Trust and Loan Companies Act continues in force notwithstanding that the entity that controls the bank, insurance company, trust company or loan company is a federal member institution the shares of which are vested in the Corporation by an order made under paragraph 39.13(1)(a).

Section.Text: Section 45.2 of the Act is replaced by the following:

All information regarding the affairs of a federal institution or provincial institution or of any person dealing therewith that is obtained or produced by or for the Corporation is confidential and shall be treated accordingly.
Section 47 of the Act is replaced by the following:

A person is guilty of an offence if the person prepares, signs, approves or concurs in any account, statement, return, report or other document required to be submitted to the Corporation under this Act, the by-laws or an application to become a member institution or a policy of deposit insurance that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: contains any false or deceptive information; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: fails to present fairly information required to be submitted to the Corporation.

Section.Text: Subsection 2(4) of the schedule to the Act is repealed.
The long title of the Canadian Payments Association Act is replaced by the following:

AmendedText.LongTitle: An Act respecting the Canadian Payments Association and the regulation of systems and arrangements for the making of payments

Section.Text: Section 1 of the Act is replaced by the following:
This Act may be cited as the Canadian Payments Act.

Subsection.Text: The definitions federation of cooperative credit societies or federation and Superintendent in subsection 2(1) of the Act are repealed.
The definition Chairman in subsection 2(1) of the English version of the Act is repealed.
The definition payment item in subsection 2(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: payment item means a bill of exchange drawn on a member and includes any other class of items approved by by-law;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: cooperative credit association means an association to which the Cooperative Credit Associations Act applies;
life insurance company means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a life company within the meaning of subsection 2(1) of the Insurance Companies Act,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a foreign life company within the meaning of section 571 of that Act, acting in respect of its insurance business in Canada, or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a body corporate that carries on under a constating instrument issued under an Act of the legislature of a province a business substantially similar to the business of a life company referred to in paragraph (a);

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: fonds mutuel en instruments du marché monétaire

Definition.Text: money market mutual fund means a fund

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the assets of which are fully or principally invested in short-term and highly liquid debt securities, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that satisfies conditions prescribed by regulation;

Definition.Text: qualified corporation means a body corporate

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that has issued shares to the public under a prospectus, filed in accordance with the laws of the province in which the shares were issued, having conditions attached to the shares requiring the body corporate to accept the surrender of any fully-paid shares, or fractions or portions of the shares,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: at the demand of their holder, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: at prices determined and payable in accordance with specified conditions,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: whose shares referred to in paragraph (a) have a fair market value, determined without regard to any voting rights attached to the shares, that is not less than 95 % of the fair market value of all of the issued shares of the capital stock of the body corporate, and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: having assets that constitute a money market mutual fund;

Definition.Text: qualified trust means an inter vivos trust

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in which the interest of its beneficiaries is described by reference to units of the trust that have been issued to the public under a prospectus, filed in accordance with the laws of the province in which the units were issued, having conditions attached to the units requiring the trustee to accept the surrender of any fully-paid units, or fractions or portions of the units,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: at the demand of their holder, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: at prices determined and payable in accordance with specified conditions,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: whose units referred to in paragraph (a) have a fair market value, determined without regard to any voting rights attached to the units, that is not less than 95 % of the fair market value of all of the issued units of the trust, and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: whose assets constitute a money market mutual fund;

Definition.Text: securities dealer means a body corporate that is registered or licensed under the laws of a province to trade in securities, as agent or for its own behalf;
trustee, in respect of a qualified trust, means a trustee that is a body corporate;
user means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in Part 1, a person who is a user of payment services but is not a member, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in Part 2, a person who is a user of services provided by a participant of a payment system but is not a participant in the system.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the English version of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: Chairperson means the Chairperson of the Board referred to in section 15;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of Part 1, a local cooperative credit society, a cooperative credit association, a central cooperative credit society or a federation of cooperative credit societies is deemed not to be a loan company or a trust company.
A rule is not a statutory instrument for the purposes of the Statutory Instruments Act.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 2:

Subsection.Text: This Act is binding on Her Majesty in right of Canada.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Her Majesty in right of a province
Subsection.Text: If Her Majesty in right of a province becomes a member of the Association, this Act is binding on Her Majesty in right of the province.

Section.Text: The heading before section 3 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The heading before section 4 of the Act is repealed.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 4(1)(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any other person who is entitled under this Part to be a member and who, on application to the Association for membership in the Association, establishes entitlement to be a member.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 4(2) to (4) of the Act are replaced by the following:
Each of the following persons is entitled to be a member of the Association if they meet the requirements set out in the regulations and the by-laws:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a central, a trust company, a loan company and any other person, other than a local that is a member of a central or a cooperative credit association, that accepts deposits transferable by order to a third party;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a federation, if the federation has as one of its members a central that is a member of the Association, except that the federation may not vote at any meeting of members of the Association;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: Her Majesty in right of a province or an agent thereof, if Her Majesty in right of the province or the agent thereof accepts deposits transferable by order to a third party;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a life insurance company;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a securities dealer;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a cooperative credit association;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the trustee of a qualified trust; and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: a qualified corporation, on behalf of its money market mutual fund.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 4(2)(b) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (2), is repealed.
Subsection 4(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

A member, other than a member referred to in paragraphs (1)(a) to (c), ceases being a member of the Association three days after the day on which the Board adopts a resolution, by a majority of not less than two-thirds of the votes cast by directors voting in respect of the resolution, declaring that, in the opinion of the Board, the member does not meet the requirements set out in the regulations and the by-laws.
Paragraph 4(7)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: eligible, despite any other provision of this Part, for readmission to the Association as a member until a resolution is adopted by the Board, by a majority of not less than two-thirds of the votes cast by directors voting in respect of the resolution, declaring that, in the opinion of the Board, the member or former member concerned meets the requirements set out in the regulations and the by-laws; or

Section.Text: Section 5 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The objects of the Association are to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: establish and operate national systems for the clearing and settlement of payments and other arrangements for the making or exchange of payments;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: facilitate the interaction of its clearing and settlement systems and related arrangements with other systems or arrangements involved in the exchange, clearing or settlement of payments; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: facilitate the development of new payment methods and technologies.

Subsection.Text: In pursuing its objects, the Association shall promote the efficiency, safety and soundness of its clearing and settlement systems and take into account the interests of users.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 6(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Association may, in carrying out its objects,

Section.Text: Section 8 of the Act is replaced by the following:
There shall be a Board of Directors of the Association consisting of sixteen persons elected or appointed in accordance with section 9.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 9(1)(b) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an officer of the Bank to be an alternate director to the director appointed under paragraph (a) and the alternate director so appointed may act as a director during any period in which the director for whom he or she is an alternate is, by reason of absence or incapacity, unable to act.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 9(2) to (4) of the Act are replaced by the following:

The Minister shall appoint three directors of the Association to hold office for a term of not more than three years, except that the first director appointed shall be appointed to hold office for a term of three years, the second shall be appointed to hold office for a term of two years and the third shall be appointed to hold office for a term of one year.
No person is eligible to be appointed under subsection (1.1) or, having been appointed under that subsection, to continue as a member of the Board who is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a director, an officer or an employee of a person that is eligible to apply to be a member of the Association, or of an affiliate of such a person;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: employed in any capacity in the public service of Canada or a province or holds any office or position for which any salary or other remuneration is payable out of public moneys; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a member of the Senate or House of Commons or a member of a provincial legislature.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (3), twelve directors of the Association shall be elected by the members to hold office for a term of three years, except that of those first elected four shall be elected for a term of three years, four for a term of two years and four for a term of one year.
For the purpose of election of directors, the members, other than the Bank of Canada, shall be grouped into seven classes, namely,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: banks and authorized foreign banks;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: centrals and cooperative credit associations;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: trust companies and loan companies;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: qualified corporations and trustees of qualified trusts;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: securities dealers;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: life insurance companies; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: other members.

Subsection.Text: If a resolution passed by a two-thirds majority of the members of a class described in subsection (3) who are present at a special meeting of members of that class called to consider the resolution provides for the recall of a director elected by that class, then, despite subsection (2), that director ceases to be a director of the Association at the time notice of the resolution is given to the director or at any other time that may be fixed by by-law.
A director appointed under subsection (1.1) shall be paid by the Association such remuneration as may be fixed by by-law.
For the purposes of paragraph (1.2)(a),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: one body corporate is affiliated with another body corporate if one of them is the subsidiary of the other or both are subsidiaries of the same body corporate or each of them is controlled by the same person; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if two bodies corporate are affiliated with the same body corporate at the same time, they are deemed to be affiliated with each other.

Subsection.Text: In paragraph (6)(a), control means control in any manner that results in control in fact, whether directly through the ownership of securities or indirectly through a trust, agreement or arrangement, the ownership of any body corporate or otherwise.

Section.Text: Section 10 of the Act is repealed.
Section 12 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

At every meeting of members at which a director is elected, the members of the class that elected the director shall elect an alternate director for that director and the alternate director so elected may act as a director during any period in which the director for whom he or she is an alternate is, by reason of absence or incapacity, unable to act.
Subsections 13(2) and (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: At a meeting of a class of members at which a director is to be elected, the members of that class present or deemed to be present, in person or by proxy, constitute a quorum if, together, they are entitled to cast a majority of the votes that may be cast for the election of a director by all members of that class.

Section.Text: The heading before section 15 of the Act is replaced by the following:
Subsection 15(3) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Chairperson or, in the absence or incapacity of the Chairperson, his or her alternate has, in the event of a tie vote on any matter before the Board at a meeting of the Board, a second vote.

Section.Text: Subsection 18(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Board may make such by-laws as it considers necessary for the attainment of the objects of the Association and in particular, but without limiting the generality of the foregoing, may make by-laws

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the procedure in all business at meetings of the Board, or its committees;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: establishing, subject to this Part, requirements for membership in the Association;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: for the administration and management of the business of the Association;

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the remuneration of directors appointed under subsection 9(1.1);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the exchange and clearing of payment items and related matters;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: respecting settlements and related matters;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the payment of dues by the members and establishing the method of determining the amount of those dues;

Paragraph.Label: (f.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the payment of fees for services performed by or on behalf of the Association and establishing the method of determining the amount of those fees;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: establishing penalties to be paid by members for failure to comply with the by-laws and rules, and procedures in respect of the imposition of penalties;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the authenticity and integrity of payment items and messages; and

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the identification and authentication of members and other persons.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 19(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to the by-laws, the Board may make such rules as it considers necessary for the attainment of the objects of the Association and, without limiting the generality of the foregoing, may make rules

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting payment items acceptable for exchange, clearing or settlement;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: establishing standards and procedures in respect of the exchange and clearing of payment items;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting settlements and related matters;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the authenticity and integrity of payment items and messages; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the identification and authentication of members and other persons.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 19(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Association shall make a copy of every rule available to members in the manner determined by the General Manager.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Copies of rules to be sent to Minister
Subsection.Text: A copy of every rule shall be sent to the Minister within ten days after it is made.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 19:
Subject to the by-laws and rules, the Board may make such statements of principle and standards as it considers necessary for the attainment of the objects of the Association.

Subsection.Text: A rule, including an amendment to or a repeal of a rule, made under subsection 19(1) shall not come into force before the thirtieth day after a copy of it is sent to the Minister in accordance with subsection 19(4), but the Minister may declare the rule to be in force at any time before that period expires.

If the Minister is of the opinion that an extension of the period mentioned in subsection (1) is necessary to permit adequate review of a rule, the Minister may within 10 days after its receipt, on written notice to the sender of the rule, extend that period by up to 30 days.
The Minister may disallow the whole or a part of a rule.
If the Minister is of the opinion that it is in the public interest to do so, the Minister may, in writing, direct the Association to make, amend or repeal a by-law, rule or standard.
Before a directive is given to the Association, the Minister shall consult the Board, and may consult any interested party, with respect to the content and effect of the directive.

As soon as is practicable after implementing a directive and completing any action required to be taken in connection with it, the Association shall notify the Minister that the directive has been implemented and the action completed.
A directive is not a statutory instrument for the purposes of the Statutory Instruments Act. However, it must be published in the Canada Gazette.
The Board shall ensure that a directive is implemented in a prompt and efficient manner.
Every director, officer and employee of the Association shall comply with any directive given to the Association.

Section.Text: For the purpose of the administration of this Part, the Association shall provide the Minister with such information and documents as the Minister may from time to time request.
The heading before section 20 of the Act is replaced by the following:
Subsection 20(2) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Chairperson is the chairperson of the Executive Committee.

Section.Text: Section 21 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Board may, subject to the regulations, establish other committees consisting of such persons as the Board considers appropriate.
Subject to the by-laws, the Board may delegate to its committees such powers and duties as the Board considers appropriate.

Subsection.Text: There shall be a Stakeholder Advisory Council consisting of not more than twenty persons appointed in accordance with subsections (3) and (4).
The object of the Council is to provide counsel and advice to the Board on payment and clearing and settlement matters and any other matter relating to the objects of the Association.
The Board shall appoint up to two directors elected under subsection 9(2) to be members of the Council.

The remaining members shall be appointed by the Board, in consultation with the Minister, for a term of not more than three years, except that as far as possible one third of the first members shall be appointed for a term of three years, one third shall be appointed for a term of two years and one third shall be appointed for a term of one year.
The Council must be broadly representative of users and service providers to payment systems.
The members of the Council shall elect a chairperson and a vice-chairperson of the Council from among the members of the Council for a term of not more than two years.

The members of the Council shall serve without remuneration but may be paid by the Association any reasonable travel and living expenses incurred by them when engaged on the business of the Council while absent from their ordinary places of residence.

Section.Text: The heading before section 27 and sections 27 and 28 of the Act are replaced by the following:
The fiscal year of the Association is the calendar year.

Subsection.Text: Subject to the by-laws, a meeting of the Board or of a committee of the Board, a meeting of the members or a meeting of the Stakeholder Advisory Council may be held by means of such telephonic, electronic or other communications facilities as permit all persons participating in the meeting to communicate adequately with each other during the meeting.
A person participating in a meeting by any means referred to in subsection (1) is deemed for the purposes of this Part to be present at the meeting.

Section.Text: Section 30 of the Act and the heading before it are repealed.
Section 33 of the Act and the heading before it are repealed.
Subsection 34(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Canada Corporations Act, chapter C-32 of the Revised Statutes of Canada, 1970, does not apply to the Association.

Section.Text: Section 35 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: establishing the number of members of committees of the Board and the number of members of the Board that shall be designated by the Board from each class of members established under subsection 9(3);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the election of directors of the Association, including regulations

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: respecting the eligibility of persons to be elected as directors,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: determining the number of directors, if any, to be elected in respect of each class established under subsection 9(3), and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: prescribing the circumstances when two or more of the classes established under subsection 9(3) are to be deemed to be one class and determining the number of directors to be elected in respect of that deemed class;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: determining the number of votes that a member is entitled to cast for the election of directors;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: establishing requirements for membership in the Association to be met by persons or classes of persons;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the conditions that a money market mutual fund must satisfy; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: generally, for carrying out the purposes and provisions of this Part.

Subsection.Text: A director elected in respect of classes that are deemed to be one class under regulations made under subparagraph (1)(b)(iii) is deemed to be elected by and to represent the members of each of the affected classes for the purposes of subsection 9(4) and sections 11 to 13.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 35:
The definitions in this section apply in this Part.

Definition.Text: designated payment system means a payment system designated under subsection 37(1).
participant means a party to an arrangement in respect of a payment system.
payment system means a system or arrangement for the exchange of messages effecting, ordering, enabling or facilitating the making of payments or transfers of value.
rule means a rule, by whatever name called, that governs a designated payment system. It includes an amendment to or a repeal of a rule.

Section.Text: This Part does not apply to the Association.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, if he or she considers that it is in the public interest to do so, designate a payment system that in the opinion of the Minister

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is national or substantially national in its scope; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: plays a major role in supporting transactions in Canadian financial markets or the Canadian economy.

Subsection.Text: The following factors shall be considered in a determination of whether it is in the public interest to designate a payment system:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the level of financial safety provided by the payment system to the participants and users;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the efficiency and competitiveness of payment systems in Canada; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the best interests of the financial system in Canada.

Subsection.Text: Before a payment system is designated, the Minister shall consult the manager and the participants of the payment system and may consult interested parties, with respect to the effect of the designation.
The Minister shall notify the manager and the participants of a designated payment system in any manner that the Minister considers appropriate.
A designation is not a statutory instrument for the purposes of the Statutory Instruments Act. However, it must be published in the Canada Gazette.

Section.MarginalNote: Copies of rules to be sent to Minister

Subsection.Text: A copy of every rule governing a designated payment system shall be sent by the manager of the designated payment system or, if there is none, by the participants to the Minister

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a rule made before the designation of the payment system, within 30 days after the designation; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a rule made after the designation, within 10 days after it is made.

Subsection.Text: A rule, other than a rule referred to in paragraph (1)(a), does not come into force before the thirtieth day after a copy of it is sent to the Minister under subsection (1), but the Minister may declare the rule to be in force at any time before that period expires.

If the Minister is of the opinion that an extension of the period mentioned in subsection (2) is necessary to permit adequate review of a rule, the Minister may within 10 days after its receipt, on written notice to the sender of the rule, extend that period by up to 30 days.
The Minister may disallow the whole or a part of a rule.
The Minister may exempt a designated payment system from the application of subsection (2).
The Minister may issue guidelines in respect of any matter relating to the administration or enforcement of this Part.
Guidelines shall be made available to the public and the Minister shall give notice of them in any manner that the Minister considers appropriate.
The Minister may issue a written directive to the manager or a participant of a designated payment system in respect of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the conditions a person must meet to become a participant in the designated payment system;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the operation of the designated payment system;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the interaction of the designated payment system with other payment systems; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the relationship of the designated payment system with users.

Subsection.Text: Before giving a directive, the Minister shall consult the person to whom it is to be given, and may consult any interested party, with respect to the content and effect of the directive.
The Minister may specify in a directive that a manager of a designated payment system or a participant shall, within such time as the Minister considers necessary,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: cease or refrain from engaging in an act or course of conduct;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: perform such acts as in the opinion of the Minister are necessary in the public interest; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: make, amend or repeal a rule.

Subsection.Text: As soon as is practicable after implementing a directive and completing any actions required to be taken in connection with it, the person to whom it is given shall notify the Minister that the directive has been implemented and the action completed.
A directive is not a statutory instrument for the purposes of the Statutory Instruments Act. However, it must be published in the Canada Gazette.
A directive is binding on the person to whom it is given.

If the Minister requires information in order to determine whether a payment system should be designated under subsection 37(1), the Minister may request that the manager of the payment system or a participant provide the Minister with any information and documents regarding the system that the Minister may require.

Every manager of a designated payment system or, if there is none, the participants shall, in respect of the designated payment system, provide the Minister with such information and documents as the Minister may from time to time request.
Every person to whom a request is directed shall comply with the request.
If a designated payment system does not have a Canadian manager, the Canadian participants

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: shall comply with the obligations imposed under this Part on a manager of a designated payment system, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: have all the rights conferred by this Part on a manager of a designated payment system

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: in the same manner and to the same extent as if the Canadian participants were the manager of the designated payment system on which those obligations and rights are imposed or conferred and, for that purpose, any action that the Minister may take in respect of the manager of the designated payment system may only be taken in respect of its Canadian participants.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Failure to comply of system manager
Subsection.Text: If a manager of a designated payment system fails to comply with the obligations imposed on it under this Part in respect of the designated payment system or otherwise contravenes this Part, the participants jointly and severally shall comply with those obligations or are liable for the contravention in the same manner and to the same extent as the manager.
In subsection (1), a manager of a designated payment system or a participant is “Canadian” if the manager or participant is incorporated or formed under the laws of Canada or a province.
Information and documents obtained under this Act are confidential and shall be treated accordingly.
Nothing in subsection (1) prevents the Minister from disclosing any information or documents to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any government agency or regulatory body charged with the regulation of financial institutions, as defined in section 2 of the Bank Act, for purposes related to that regulation,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Bank of Canada or any officer of the Bank authorized in writing by the Governor of the Bank, and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Chairperson of the Canada Deposit Insurance Corporation or any officer of that Corporation authorized in writing by the Chairperson,

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: if the Minister is satisfied that the information or documents will be treated as confidential by the agency, body or person to whom they are disclosed.

Section.MarginalNote: No liability if in good faith
Section.Text: No action lies against Her Majesty, the Minister, any officer or employee of the Department of Finance or any person acting under the direction of the Minister for anything done or omitted to be done in good faith in the administration or discharge of any powers or duties that under this Act are intended or authorized to be executed or performed.

If a person fails to comply with a provision of this Act or a directive issued to it by the Minister under subsection 19.3(1) or 40(1) in connection with any matter under this Act, or a person to whom a request referred to in section 41 is directed fails to comply with the request, the Minister may apply to a superior court for an order directing the person to comply with the provision, directive or request and, on the application, the court may so order and make any further order it thinks fit.

On an application for judicial review under the Federal Court Act of a designation under subsection 37(1) or of a directive issued under subsection 19.3(1) or 40(1), no stay of the designation or directive shall be granted pending the final disposition of the application.
Every person who, without reasonable cause, contravenes any provision of this Act is guilty of an offence and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a natural person, liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding $100,000 or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding twelve months, or to both; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of any other entity, liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding $500,000.

Section.Text: The English version of the Act is amended by replacing the word “Chairman” with the word “Chairperson” wherever it occurs in the following provisions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 15(1) and (2);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 16(2);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 19(2);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 20(1); and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 20(3).

Section.Text: The headings before sections 5, 7, 8, 16, 17, 22, 25, 26, 29, 31, 34 and 35 of the Act are converted from roman to italic type to conform with the format of the new Parts enacted by this Act.

Subsection.Text: The person who holds the office of Chairperson of the Board of the Canadian Payments Association immediately before section 8 of the Canadian Payments Act, as amended by section 218 of this Act, comes into force continues in office as the Chairperson of the Board for the remainder of the term for which that person was appointed Chairperson.

Each person who holds office as a director of the Canadian Payments Association immediately before the day subsection 9(2) of the Canadian Payments Act, as amended by subsection 219(2) of this Act, comes into force ceases to hold office at the close of the next annual meeting of the members of the Association that is held after the coming into force of that subsection. The new directors of the Board shall be elected at that meeting.

Section.Text: Every rule of the Canadian Payments Association made under the Canadian Payments Association Act before the coming into force of subsection 2(3) of that Act, as enacted by section 219 of this Act, is deemed to have come into force on the day the rule was made.

Subsection.Text: The definition subsidiary in section 2 of the Cooperative Credit Associations Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: subsidiary means an entity that is a subsidiary of another entity within the meaning of section 5;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (d) of the definition financial institution in section 2 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an insurance company or a fraternal benefit society incorporated or formed under the Insurance Companies Act,

Subsection.Text: Section 2 of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: Agency means the Financial Consumer Agency of Canada established under section 3 of the Financial Consumer Agency of Canada Act;
branch, in respect of an association, means an agency, the head office and any other office of the association;
Commissioner means the Commissioner of the Financial Consumer Agency of Canada appointed under section 4 of the Financial Consumer Agency of Canada Act;
consumer provision means a provision referred to in paragraph (b) of the definition consumer provision in section 2 of the Financial Consumer Agency of Canada Act;

league means a cooperative corporation incorporated by or under an Act of the legislature of a province whose membership consists wholly or primarily of local cooperative credit societies and whose principal purpose is the provision of administrative, technical, research and consultative services, and goods related to those services, to any cooperative credit society or to persons intending to organize or operate such a society;
retail association, for the purpose of any particular provision of this Act, means an association as defined in the regulations;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 3(1)(e) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: e)
Paragraph.Text: dans tous les cas, la personne dont l’influence directe ou indirecte auprès de l’entité est telle que son exercice aurait pour résultat le contrôle de fait de celle-ci.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 3(3) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
A person is deemed to control, within the meaning of paragraph (1)(a), (b) or (d), an entity if the aggregate of
Section 3 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

The Minister may, for any purpose of any provision of this Act that refers to control within the meaning of paragraph (1)(e), make guidelines respecting what constitutes such control, including guidelines describing the policy objectives that the guidelines and the relevant provisions of the Act are intended to achieve and, if any such guidelines are made, the reference to paragraph (1)(e) in that provision shall be interpreted in accordance with the guidelines.

Section.Text: Sections 4 and 5 of the Act are replaced by the following:
A body corporate is the holding body corporate of any entity that is its subsidiary.
An entity is a subsidiary of another entity if it is controlled by the other entity.
Subsection 6(2) of the Act is repealed.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 13:

A reference in any other Act of Parliament to “an association to which the Cooperative Credit Associations Act applies” is to be construed as not including a central cooperative credit society for which an order has been made under subsection 473(1).
Section 14 of the Act is replaced by the following:
This Act applies to the former-Act association, and to every body corporate incorporated or formed by or under this Act, so long as it is not discontinued under this Act.
Section 22 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2), associations shall not carry on business after the day that is five years after this section comes into force, except that if Parliament dissolves on that day or at any time within the three-month period before that day, associations may continue to carry on business until the day that is one hundred and eighty days after the first day of the first session of the next Parliament.
The Governor in Council may, by order, extend by up to six months the time during which associations may continue to carry on business. No more than one order may be made under this subsection.

Section.Text: The heading of Part III of the Act is replaced by the following:
Sections 23 and 24 of the Act are replaced by the following:
On the application of one or more persons made in accordance with this Act, the Minister may, subject to this Part, issue letters patent incorporating an association.
An application for incorporation of an association may only be made by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an association; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: persons who are entitled to be members of an association under Part IV that include at least

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: two central cooperative credit societies not all of which are incorporated under the laws of one province,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: ten local cooperative credit societies not all of which are incorporated under the laws of one province, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: two or more leagues not all of which are incorporated under the laws of one province.

Section.Text: Section 27 of the Act is replaced by the following:
Before issuing letters patent to incorporate an association, the Minister shall take into account all matters that the Minister considers relevant to the application, including

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the nature and sufficiency of the financial resources of the applicant or applicants as a source of continuing financial support for the association;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the soundness and feasibility of the plans of the applicant or applicants for the future conduct and development of the business of the association;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the business record and experience of the applicant or applicants;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the character and integrity of the applicant or applicants or, if the applicant or any of the applicants is a body corporate, its reputation for being operated in a manner that is consistent with the standards of good character and integrity;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: whether the association will be operated responsibly by persons with the competence and experience suitable for involvement in the operation of a financial institution;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the impact of any integration of the businesses and operations of the applicant or applicants with those of the association on the conduct of those businesses and operations;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: whether the association is to be operated in accordance with cooperative principles; and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the best interests of the financial system in Canada and, in particular, the cooperative financial system in Canada.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 31:

Subsection.Text: A body corporate incorporated under the Canada Business Corporations Act or any other Act of Parliament may apply to the Minister for letters patent continuing the body corporate as an association.

A body corporate incorporated otherwise than by or under an Act of Parliament may, if so authorized by the laws of the jurisdiction where it is incorporated, apply to the Minister for letters patent continuing the body corporate as an association.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Continuance for the purpose of amalgamation
Subsection.Text: A body corporate incorporated or continued otherwise than under this Act may apply to the Minister for letters patent continuing the body corporate as an association if the body corporate proposes to be continued under this Act for the purpose of amalgamating with another body corporate in compliance with this Act.
When a body corporate applies for letters patent under section 31.1, sections 24 to 27 apply in respect of the application, with any modifications that the circumstances require.
When a body corporate applies for letters patent under section 31.1, the application must be duly authorized by a special resolution.
A copy of the special resolution must be filed with the application.

On the application of a body corporate under subsection 31.1(1) or (2), the Minister may, subject to this Part, issue letters patent continuing the body corporate as an association if the body corporate

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: satisfies the requirements for incorporation as an association or will, immediately after the letters patent are issued, satisfy those requirements;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is organized and operated and carries on its business on a cooperative basis or will, immediately after the letters patent are issued, be organized and operated and carry on its business on a cooperative basis; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: has a capital and corporate structure that, if set out in the letters patent and by-laws, would meet the requirements of this Act.

Subsection.Text: On the application of a body corporate under subsection 31.1(3), the Minister may, subject to this Part, issue letters patent continuing the body corporate as an association only if the Minister is of the opinion that the association that results from the amalgamation will

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: satisfy the requirements for incorporation as an association;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: be organized and operated and carry on its business on a cooperative basis; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: have a capital and corporate structure that meet the requirements of this Act.

Subsection.Text: If letters patent are issued to a body corporate under subsection (1) or (2), section 27 applies in respect of the issue of letters patent, with any modifications that the circumstances require.

Section.Text: On the day set out in the letters patent continuing a body corporate as an association under section 31.3,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the body corporate becomes an association as if it had been incorporated under this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the letters patent are deemed to be the incorporating instrument of the continued company.

Subsection.Text: When a body corporate is continued as an association under section 31.3, the Superintendent shall without delay send a copy of the letters patent to the appropriate official or public body in the jurisdiction in which the body corporate was incorporated.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Notice of issuance of letters patent
Subsection.Text: The Superintendent shall cause to be published in the Canada Gazette a notice of the issuance of letters patent continuing a body corporate as an association.

Section.Text: Where a body corporate is continued as an association,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the property of the body corporate continues to be the property of the association;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the association continues to be liable for the obligations of the body corporate;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an existing cause of action or claim by or against the body corporate or any liability of the body corporate to prosecution is unaffected;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a civil, criminal or administrative action or proceeding pending by or against the body corporate may continue to be prosecuted by or against the association;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a conviction against, or any ruling, order or judgment in favour of or against the body corporate may be enforced by or against the association;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a person who, on the day the body corporate becomes an association, was the holder of a security issued by the body corporate is not deprived of any right or privilege available to the person at that time in respect of the security or relieved of any liability in respect of the security, but any such right or privilege may be exercised only in accordance with this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the by-laws of the body corporate, except those that are in conflict with this Act, continue as the by-laws of the association.

Subsection.Text: Despite any other provision of this Act or the regulations, the Minister may, on the recommendation of the Superintendent, by order, grant to an association in respect of which letters patent were issued under section 31.3 permission to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: engage in a business activity specified in the order that an association is not otherwise permitted by this Act to engage in and that the body corporate continued as the association was engaging in at the time the application for the letters patent was made;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: continue to have issued and outstanding debt obligations the issue of which is not authorized by this Act if the debt obligations were outstanding at the time the application for the letters patent was made;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: hold assets that an association is not otherwise permitted by this Act to hold if the assets were held by the body corporate continued as the association at the time the application for the letters patent was made;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: acquire and hold assets that an association is not otherwise permitted by this Act to acquire or hold if the body corporate continued as the association was obliged, at the time the application for the letters patent was made, to acquire those assets; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: maintain outside Canada any records or registers required by this Act to be maintained in Canada and maintain and process outside Canada information and data relating to the preparation and maintenance of those records or registers.

Subsection.Text: The permission shall be expressed to be granted for a period specified in the order not exceeding

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: with respect to any activity described in paragraph (1)(a), thirty days after the date of issue of the letters patent or, if the activity is conducted under an agreement existing on the date of issue of the letters patent, the expiration of the agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: with respect to any matter described in paragraph (1)(b), ten years; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: with respect to any matter described in any of paragraphs (1)(c) to (e), two years.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (4), the Minister may, on the recommendation of the Superintendent, by order, renew a permission with respect to any matter described in paragraphs (1)(b) to (d) for any further period or periods that the Minister considers necessary.
The Minister shall not grant to an association a permission

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: with respect to matters described in paragraph (1)(b), that purports to be effective more than ten years after the date of the approval for the association to commence and carry on business, unless the Minister is satisfied on the basis of evidence on oath provided by an officer of the association that the association will not be able at law to redeem at the end of the ten years the outstanding debt obligations to which the permission relates; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: with respect to matters referred to in paragraphs (1)(c) and (d), that purports to be effective more than ten years after the date of the approval for the association to commence and carry on business.

Section.Text: Sections 32 to 34 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: An association may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: apply for letters patent continuing the association as a company under subsection 33(1) of the Trust and Loan Companies Act or amalgamating and continuing the association as a company under section 228 and subsection 234(1) of that Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: apply for letters patent continuing the association as a bank under subsection 35(1) of the Bank Act or amalgamating and continuing the association as a bank under section 223 and subsection 229(1) of that Act;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: apply for letters patent continuing the association as a bank holding company under subsection 684(1) of the Bank Act or amalgamating and continuing the association as a bank holding company under section 803 and subsection 809(1) of that Act;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: with the approval of the Minister, apply for a certificate of continuance under section 187 of the Canada Business Corporations Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: with the approval of the Minister, apply for a certificate of continuance, or a certificate of continuance and a certificate of amalgamation, under section 285 of the Canada Cooperatives Act.

Subsection.Text: No approval referred to in paragraph (1)(d) or (e) may be given to an association unless the Minister is satisfied that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the application has been authorized by a special resolution of the members; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the association holds no deposits that are insured under the Canada Deposit Insurance Corporation Act.

Section.MarginalNote: Effect of letters patent or certificate
Section.Text: On the day specified in the letters patent or certificate of continuance referred to in any of paragraphs 32(1)(a) to (e), the Act referred to in the relevant paragraph applies and this Act ceases to apply to the body corporate continued under that Act.

If a special resolution authorizing the application for letters patent or a certificate of continuance referred to in any of paragraphs 32(1)(a) to (e) so states, the directors of an association may, without further approval of the members, withdraw the application before it is acted on.
Section 36 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The name of an association shall include

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the word “cooperative” or “coopérative”, along with another word or expression indicating the financial nature of the association,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the phrase “central credit union”, “credit union central” or “fédération de caisses populaires”, or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any word or phrase specified by the Minister

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: or any combination or derivative thereof.

Section.Text: Section 37 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Despite section 35, an association that is affiliated with another entity may, with the consent of that entity and the approval of the Superintendent, be incorporated with, or change its name to, substantially the same name as that of the affiliated entity.
Subsection 40(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If an association has been directed under subsection (1) to change its name and has not, within sixty days after the service of the direction, changed its name to a name that is not prohibited by this Act, the Superintendent may revoke the name of the association and assign to it a name and, until changed in accordance with section 219 or 221, the name of the association is thereafter the name so assigned.

Section.Text: Subsection 41(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Only a person that is an association, a central cooperative credit society, a local cooperative credit society, a cooperative corporation, a league, a deposit protection agency or an unincorporated organization consisting wholly of any of those entities may be admitted to membership in an association.

Section.Text: Subsection 41(3) of the Act is repealed.
Section 50 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The membership in an association must include at least

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an association;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: two central cooperative credit societies not all of which are incorporated under the laws of one province;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: ten local cooperative credit societies not all of which are incorporated under the laws of one province; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: two or more leagues not all of which are incorporated under the laws of one province.

Subsection.Text: If, at any time, the membership in an association is not in accordance with subsection (1), the association shall without delay take the steps that are necessary to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: apply for a certificate of continuation or letters patent referred to in subsection 32(1); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: liquidate and dissolve the association under Part VII.

Section.Text: Section 52 of the Act is replaced by the following:
No person other than an association may control an association.
Paragraph 60(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the association has paid-in capital of at least five million dollars or such greater amount as the Minister may specify;

Section.Text: Section 61 of the Act is replaced by the following:
An order approving the commencement and carrying on of business by an association may contain any conditions or limitations that the Superintendent considers appropriate.
Subsection 70(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A by-law referred to in subsection (1) must be approved by special resolution.

Section.Text: Section 74 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: The by-laws of an association may, with the approval of the Superintendent, provide for a formula or procedure for valuing a member of the association or any of its assets or liabilities when the member, or the asset or liability, is proposed to be acquired by the association in exchange for membership shares or shares of the association. The approval of the Superintendent under subsection (1) is not necessary when such shares are issued in accordance with such a by-law.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 75(2.1) of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (2), an association may, subject to subsection (2.2), record in the appropriate stated capital account part of the amount of any consideration it receives for shares it issues

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in exchange for

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: property of a person who immediately before the exchange did not deal with the association at arm’s length within the meaning of that expression in the Income Tax Act or property of any other prescribed person, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: shares of, or another interest in, a body corporate that immediately before the exchange, or because of the exchange, did not deal with the association at arm’s length within the meaning of that expression in the Income Tax Act or shares of or another interest in any prescribed entity; or

Subsection.Text: Subsection 86(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The directors of an association may declare and an association may pay a dividend by issuing fully paid membership shares or options or rights to acquire membership shares to members or fully paid shares or options or rights to acquire fully paid shares to members or shareholders and, subject to subsections (4) and (5), the directors of an association may declare and an association may pay a dividend in money or property, and if a dividend is to be paid in money, the dividend may be paid in a currency other than the currency of Canada.
Section 86 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

Subsection.MarginalNote: When dividend not to be declared
Subsection.Text: The directors of an association shall not declare and an association shall not pay a dividend in any financial year without the approval of the Superintendent if, on the day the dividend is declared, the total of all dividends declared by the association in that year would exceed the aggregate of the association’s net income up to that day in that year and its retained net income for the preceding two financial years.

Section.Text: Subsection 151(2) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: La présence à l’assemblée équivaut à une renonciation de l’avis de convocation, sauf lorsque la personne y assiste spécialement pour s’opposer aux délibérations au motif que l’assemblée n’est pas régulièrement convoquée.
Subsection 154(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

An association shall prepare a list, which may be in electronic form, of its members entitled to receive notice of a meeting under paragraph 146(1)(a), arranged in alphabetical order, which list must be prepared at the close of business on the day immediately preceding the day on which notice is given.
The portion of subsection 154(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

An association shall prepare a list, which may be in electronic form, of its shareholders entitled to receive notice of a meeting under paragraph 146(1)(b), arranged in alphabetical order and showing the number of shares held by each shareholder, which list must be prepared

Section.Text: Subsection 167(2) of the Act is amended by striking out the word “and” at the end of paragraph (d) and by adding the following after paragraph (e):

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a retail association, establish procedures to provide disclosure of information to customers of the association that is required to be disclosed by this Act and for dealing with complaints as required by section 385.22; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: designate a committee of the board of directors to monitor the procedures referred to in paragraph (f) and satisfy itself that they are being adhered to by the retail association.

Section.Text: Subsection 169(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: At least two thirds of the directors of an association must be, at the time of each director’s election or appointment, resident Canadians.
