Paragraph.Text: the business record and experience of the applicant or applicants;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the character and integrity of the applicant or applicants or, if the applicant or any of the applicants is a body corporate, its reputation for being operated in a manner that is consistent with the standards of good character and integrity;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: whether the company will be operated responsibly by persons with the competence and experience suitable for involvement in the operation of a financial institution;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the impact of any integration of the businesses and operations of the applicant or applicants with those of the company on the conduct of those businesses and operations; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the best interests of the financial system in Canada.

Section.Text: Section 38 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A company may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: apply, with the approval in writing of the Minister, for a certificate of continuance under section 187 of the Canada Business Corporations Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: apply for letters patent continuing the company as a bank under subsection 35(1) of the Bank Act or amalgamating and continuing the company as a bank under subsections 223(1) and 229(1) of that Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: apply for letters patent continuing the company as an association under subsection 31.1(1) of the Cooperative Credit Associations Act if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the company’s shareholders are limited to entities incorporated or formed by or under an Act of Parliament or of the legislature of a province that are, in the opinion of the Minister, operating as credit unions or cooperative associations, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the application for letters patent complies with prescribed terms and conditions, if any are prescribed.

Subsection.Text: No approval referred to in paragraph (1)(a) may be given to a company unless the Minister is satisfied that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the application has been authorized by a special resolution;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the company

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: holds no deposits that are insured under the Canada Deposit Insurance Corporation Act, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is not carrying on any of the fiduciary activities referred to in section 412;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: unless authorized pursuant to section 48, the company will not use the word “fiduciaire”, “fiduciary”, “fiducie”, “loan”, “loanco”, “prêt”, “trust” or “trustco” in its name after a certificate of continuance in respect of the company is issued under the Canada Business Corporations Act.

Section.Text: Section 43 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Despite section 41, a company that is affiliated with another entity may, with the consent of that entity and the approval of the Superintendent, be incorporated with, or change its name to, substantially the same name as that of the affiliated entity.
Subsection 46(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If a company has been directed under subsection (1) to change its name and has not, within sixty days after the service of the direction, changed its name to a name that is not prohibited by this Act, the Superintendent may revoke the name of the company and assign to it a name and, until changed in accordance with section 220 or 222, the name of the company is thereafter the name so assigned.

Section.Text: Section 48 of the Act is replaced by the following:
Despite subsections 47(1) and (2), a subsidiary of a company may use the company’s name in its name.
Subsection 50(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If at least five million dollars, or any greater amount that the Minister may specify, has been received by a company in respect of which letters patent were issued under section 21 from the issue of its shares, the directors of the company shall without delay call a meeting of the shareholders of the company.

Section.Text: Paragraph 56(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the company has paid-in capital of at least five million dollars or any greater amount that is specified by the Minister under subsection 50(1);

Section.Text: Subsection 64(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A by-law referred to in subsection (1) is not effective until it is confirmed or confirmed with amendments by special resolution of the shareholders at the meeting referred to in subsection (2).
Subsection 82(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The directors of a company may declare and a company may pay a dividend by issuing fully paid shares of the company or options or rights to acquire fully paid shares of the company and, subject to subsections (4) and (5), the directors of a company may declare and a company may pay a dividend in money or property, and where a dividend is to be paid in money, the dividend may be paid in a currency other than the currency of Canada.
Section 82 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

Subsection.MarginalNote: When dividend not to be declared
Subsection.Text: The directors of a company shall not declare and a company shall not pay a dividend in any financial year without the approval of the Superintendent if, on the day the dividend is declared, the total of all dividends declared by the company in that year would exceed the aggregate of the company’s net income up to that day in that year and of its retained net income for the preceding two financial years.

Section.Text: Subsection 145(2) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: La présence à l’assemblée équivaut à une renonciation de l’avis de convocation, sauf lorsque la personne y assiste spécialement pour s’opposer aux délibérations au motif que l’assemblée n’est pas régulièrement convoquée.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 148(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A company shall prepare a list, which may be in electronic form, of its shareholders entitled to receive notice of a meeting under paragraph 141(1)(a), arranged in alphabetical order and showing the number of shares held by each shareholder, which list must be prepared

Section.Text: Paragraph 161(2)(f) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: f)
Paragraph.Text: désigner l’un des comités du conseil d’administration pour surveiller l’application des mécanismes et procédures visés à l’alinéa e) et s’assurer que ces mécanismes et procédures soient respectés par la société;

Section.Text: Subsection 163(2) of the Act is replaced by the following.

Subsection.Text: At least one half of the directors of a company that is a subsidiary of a foreign institution or of a prescribed holding body corporate of a foreign institution and at least two thirds of the directors of any other company must be, at the time of each director’s election or appointment, resident Canadians.

Section.Text: Subsection 167(2) of the Act, as amended by section 351 of An Act to amend certain laws relating to financial institutions, being chapter 15 of the Statutes of Canada, 1997, is repealed.
Subsection 176(1) of the Act is amended by striking out the word “or” at the end of paragraph (c), by adding the word “or” at the end of paragraph (d) and by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: when the director is removed from office under section 509.1 or 509.2.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 187:

Subsection.Text: The directors of a company shall not transact business at a meeting of directors unless at least one of the directors who is not affiliated with the company is present.

Despite subsection (1), the directors of a company may transact business at a meeting of directors if a director who is not affiliated with the company and who is not able to be present approves, in writing or by telephonic, electronic or other communications facilities, the business transacted at the meeting.

Subsection (1) does not apply if all the voting shares of the company, other than directors’ qualifying shares, if any, are beneficially owned by a Canadian financial institution incorporated by or under an Act of Parliament.

Section.Text: Paragraph 199(3)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: review those procedures and their effectiveness in ensuring that the company is complying with Part XI;

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: if a widely held bank holding company or a widely held insurance holding company has a significant interest in any class of shares of the company,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: establish policies for entering into transactions referred to in subsection 483.1(1), and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: review transactions referred to in subsection 483.3(1); and

Section.Text: The portion of section 216 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
A director, an officer or an employee of a company is not liable under subsection 162(1) or (2) or section 212 or 215 or subsection 494(1) if the director, officer or employee relies in good faith on
The portion of subsection 217(1) of the French version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: La société peut indemniser ses administrateurs ou ses dirigeants — ou leurs prédécesseurs —, ainsi que les personnes qui, à sa demande, agissent ou ont agi en cette qualité pour une entité dont elle est ou a été actionnaire ou créancière, de tous leurs frais, y compris les montants versés en règlement d’une action ou pour satisfaire à un jugement, entraînés par des procédures civiles, pénales ou administratives auxquelles ils étaient parties en cette qualité, sauf à l’occasion d’actions intentées par la société ou pour son compte en vue d’obtenir un jugement favorable, si :

Section.Text: Section 220 of the Act is replaced by the following:

On the application of a company duly authorized by special resolution, the Minister may approve a proposal to add, change or remove any provision that is permitted by this Act to be set out in the incorporating instrument of the company.
Subsection 221(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: On receipt of an application referred to in section 220, the Minister may issue letters patent to effect the proposal.
Subsection 222(1) of the Act is amended by striking out the word “or” at the end of paragraph (i) and by adding the following after paragraph (i):

Paragraph.Label: (i.1)
Paragraph.Text: change the name of the company; or

Subsection.Text: Subsection 222(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

A by-law, or an amendment to or a repeal of a by-law, made under subsection (1) is not effective until it is confirmed or confirmed as amended by the shareholders under subsection (2) and, in the case of by-laws referred to in paragraph (1)(i.1), approved by the Superintendent.

Section.Text: Subsection 226(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2), a director or a shareholder who is entitled to vote at an annual meeting of shareholders of a company may, in accordance with sections 146 and 147, make a proposal to make an application referred to in section 220 or to make, amend or repeal the by-laws referred to in subsection 222(1) of the company.

Section.Text: Subsection 233(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If two or more bodies corporate, none of which is a company, apply for letters patent under subsection (1), sections 22 to 25 apply in respect of the application with any modifications that the circumstances require.

Before issuing letters patent of amalgamation continuing the applicants as one company, the Minister shall take into account all matters that the Minister considers relevant to the application, including

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the sources of continuing financial support for the amalgamated company;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the soundness and feasibility of the plans of the applicants for the future conduct and development of the business of the amalgamated company;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the business record and experience of the applicants;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the reputation of the applicants for being operated in a manner that is consistent with the standards of good character and integrity;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: whether the amalgamated company will be operated responsibly by persons with the competence and experience suitable for involvement in the operation of a financial institution;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the impact of any integration of the operations and businesses of the applicants on the conduct of those operations and businesses; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the best interests of the financial system in Canada.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 234:

Subsection.Text: If a company or any director, officer, employee or agent of a company is contravening or has failed to comply with any term or condition made in respect of the issuance of letters patent of amalgamation, the Minister may, in addition to any other action that may be taken under this Act, apply to a court for an order directing the company or the director, officer, employee or agent to comply with the term or condition, cease the contravention or do any thing that is required to be done, and on the application the court may so order and make any other order it thinks fit.
An appeal from an order of a court under this section lies in the same manner as, and to the same court to which, an appeal may be taken from any other order of the court.
Section 244 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (5):

A company may make the information contained in records referred to in subsection 243(1) available to persons by any system of mechanical or electronic data processing or any other information storage device that is capable of reproducing the records in intelligible written form within a reasonable time.
Subsection 244(6) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:
Les actionnaires peuvent sur demande et sans frais, une fois par année civile, obtenir un exemplaire des règlements administratifs de la société.

Section.Text: Subsection 250(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (3), a company shall maintain and process in Canada any information or data relating to the preparation and maintenance of the records referred to in section 243 unless the Superintendent has, subject to any terms and conditions that the Superintendent considers appropriate, exempted the company from the application of this section.

Section.Text: Subsection 253(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subsections 244(5) and (5.1) and sections 245 and 247 to 250 apply, with any modifications that the circumstances require, in respect of a central securities register.
The portion of paragraph 313(3)(a) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a list of the subsidiaries of the company, other than subsidiaries that are not required to be listed by the regulations and subsidiaries acquired pursuant to section 457 or pursuant to a realization of security in accordance with section 458 and which the company would not otherwise be permitted to hold, showing, with respect to each subsidiary,

Subsection.Text: Section 313 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):
The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting subsidiaries that are not required to be listed for the purposes of paragraph (3)(a).

Section.Text: Section 317 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2), a company shall send to the Superintendent a copy of the documents referred to in subsections 313(1) and (3) not later than twenty-one days before the date of each annual meeting of shareholders of the company.

If a company’s shareholders sign a resolution under paragraph 155(1)(b) in lieu of an annual meeting, the company shall send a copy of the documents referred to in subsections 313(1) and (3) to the Superintendent not later than thirty days after the signing of the resolution.
Subsection 320(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Within fifteen days after the appointment of a firm of accountants as auditor of a company, the company and the firm of accountants shall jointly designate a member of the firm who meets the qualifications described in subsection (1) to conduct the audit of the company on behalf of the firm and the company shall forthwith notify the Superintendent in writing of the designation.
Subsection 320(4) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Si, pour une raison quelconque, le membre désigné cesse de remplir ses fonctions, la société et le cabinet de comptables peuvent désigner conjointement un autre membre qui remplit les conditions du paragraphe (1); la société en avise sans délai par écrit le surintendant.

Section.Text: Subsection 374(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Nothing in subsection (1) prejudices or affects the priority of any holder of any security interest in any property of a company.

Section.Text: Subsections 375(3) to (5) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: On application by a company, the Superintendent may exempt from the application of this section and section 376 any class of non-voting shares of the company if the aggregate book value of the shares of the class is not more than 30 per cent of the aggregate book value of all the outstanding shares of the company.

Section.Text: Section 375.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: No acquisition of control without approval
Section.Text: No person shall acquire control, within the meaning of paragraph 3(1)(d), of a company, without the prior approval of the Minister.
Subsection 378(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Despite sections 375 and 376, the approval of the Minister is not required if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Superintendent has, by order, directed the company to increase its capital and shares of the company are issued and acquired in accordance with such terms and conditions as may be specified in the order; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a person who controls, within the meaning of paragraph 3(1)(a), the company acquires additional shares of the company.

Section.Text: Section 379 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every company shall, from and after the day determined under this section in respect of that company, have, and continue to have, voting shares that carry at least 35 per cent of the voting rights attached to all of the outstanding voting shares of the company and that are

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: shares of one or more classes of shares that are listed and posted for trading on a recognized stock exchange in Canada; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: shares none of which is beneficially owned by a person who is a major shareholder of the company in respect of the voting shares of the company or by any entity that is controlled by a person who is a major shareholder of the company in respect of such shares.

Subsection.Text: If the company has equity of one billion dollars or more on the day it comes into existence, the day referred to in subsection (1) is the day that is three years after that day and, in the case of any other company, the day referred to in subsection (1) is the day that is three years after the day of the first annual meeting of the shareholders of the company held after the equity of the company first reaches one billion dollars.

If general market conditions so warrant and the Minister is satisfied that a company has used its best efforts to be in compliance with this section on the day determined under subsection (2), the Minister may specify a later day as the day from and after which the company must comply with subsection (1).

Section.Text: Subsection 380(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Unless an exemption order with respect to the company is granted under section 382, if a company fails to comply with section 379 in any month, the Minister may, by order, require the company not to have, until it complies with that section, average total assets in any three month period ending on the last day of a subsequent month exceeding the company’s average total assets in the three month period ending on the last day of the month immediately before the month specified in the order.
The portion of subsection 382(1) of the Act before paragraph (f) is replaced by the following:
An entity that controls a company and that is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a widely held bank,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a bank that would be in compliance with section 379 if it were a company,

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: a widely held bank holding company,

Paragraph.Label: (b.2)
Paragraph.Text: a bank holding company that would be in compliance with section 379 if it were a company,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a company that is in compliance with section 379,

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an insurance company, other than a mutual company, to which the Insurance Companies Act applies that would be in compliance with section 379 if it were a company,

Paragraph.Label: (d.1)
Paragraph.Text: a mutual company or a fraternal benefit society to which the Insurance Companies Act applies,

Paragraph.Label: (d.2)
Paragraph.Text: an insurance holding company that would be in compliance with section 379 if it were a company,

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: an association to which the Cooperative Credit Associations Act applies,

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 382(5)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the entity that applied for the exemption order ceases to control the company;

Section.Text: Sections 384 to 386 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2) and sections 376 and 385, section 379 does not apply in respect of a company if a person acquires control of a company with equity of one billion dollars or more through the purchase or other acquisition of all or any number of the shares of the company by the person or by any entity controlled by the person.

Subsection (1) applies only if the person provides the Minister with an undertaking satisfactory to the Minister to do all things necessary so that, within three years after the acquisition, or any other period that the Minister may specify, the company has voting shares that carry at least 35 per cent of the voting rights attached to all of the outstanding voting shares of the company and that are

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: shares of one or more classes of shares that are listed and posted for trading on a recognized stock exchange in Canada; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: shares none of which is beneficially owned by a person who is a major shareholder of the company in respect of the voting shares of the company or by any entity that is controlled by a person who is a major shareholder of the company in respect of such shares.

Section.Text: At the expiration of the period for compliance with an undertaking referred to in subsection 384(2), section 379 shall apply in respect of the company to which the undertaking relates.

Subsection.Text: If, with respect to any company, a particular person contravenes section 375 or 375.1 or fails to comply with an undertaking referred to in subsection 384(2) or with any term or condition imposed under section 389, no person, and no entity controlled by the particular person, shall, in person or by proxy, exercise any voting rights

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that are attached to shares of the company beneficially owned by the particular person or any entity controlled by the particular person; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that are subject to an agreement entered into by the particular person, or any entity controlled by the particular person, pertaining to the exercise of the voting rights.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) ceases to apply in respect of a person when, as the case may be,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the shares to which the contravention relates have been disposed of;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the person ceases to control the company within the meaning of paragraph 3(1)(d);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the person failed to comply with an undertaking referred to in subsection 384(2), the company complies with section 379; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if the person failed to comply with a term or condition imposed under section 389, the person complies with the term or condition.

Section.Text: Section 387 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: An application for an approval of the Minister required under this Part must be filed with the Superintendent and contain the information, material and evidence that the Superintendent may require.

If, with respect to any particular transaction, this Part applies to more than one person, any one of those persons may make the application to the Minister for approval on behalf of all of those persons.

Section.Text: Subsection 388(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2), if an application for an approval under section 375 is made, the Minister, in determining whether or not to approve the transaction, shall take into account all matters that the Minister considers relevant to the application, including

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the nature and sufficiency of the financial resources of the applicant or applicants as a source of continuing financial support for the company;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the soundness and feasibility of the plans of the applicant or applicants for the future conduct and development of the business of the company;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the business record and experience of the applicant or applicants;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the character and integrity of the applicant or applicants or, if the applicant or any of the applicants is a body corporate, its reputation for being operated in a manner that is consistent with the standards of good character and integrity;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: whether the company will be operated responsibly by persons with the competence and experience suitable for involvement in the operation of a financial institution;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the impact of any integration of the businesses and operations of the applicant or applicants with those of the company on the conduct of those businesses and operations; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the best interests of the financial system in Canada.

Section.Text: Section 389 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Minister may impose any terms and conditions in respect of an approval given under this Part that the Minister considers necessary to ensure compliance with any provision of this Act.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 390(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If, in the opinion of the Superintendent, an application filed under this Part contains all the required information, the Superintendent shall without delay refer the application to the Minister and send a receipt to the applicant certifying the date on which the completed application was received by the Superintendent.
Subsection 390(2) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

If, in the opinion of the Superintendent, an application filed under this Part is incomplete, the Superintendent shall send a notice to the applicant specifying the information required by the Superintendent to complete the application.
Subsection 401(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If, with respect to any company, a person contravenes section 375 or 375.1 or fails to comply with an undertaking referred to in subsection 384(2) or with any terms and conditions imposed under section 389, the Minister may, if the Minister deems it in the public interest to do so, by order, direct that person and any person controlled by that person to dispose of any number of shares of the company beneficially owned by any of those persons that the Minister specifies in the order, within the time specified in the order and in the proportion, if any, as between the person and the persons controlled by that person that is specified in the order.
Subsection 401(4) of the Act is repealed.
The portion of subsection 410(1) of the Act before paragraph (e) is replaced by the following:
In addition, a company may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: act as an agent for vendors, purchasers, mortgagors, mortgagees, lessors or lessees of real property and provide consulting or appraisal services in respect of real property;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: hold, manage and otherwise deal with real property;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: outside Canada, or with the prior written approval of the Minister, in Canada, engage in any of the following activities, namely,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: collecting, manipulating and transmitting

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: information that is primarily financial or economic in nature,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: information that relates to the business of a permitted entity, as defined in subsection 449(1), or

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: any other information that the Minister may, by order, specify,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: providing advisory or other services in the design, development or implementation of information management systems,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: designing, developing or marketing computer software, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: designing, developing, manufacturing or selling, as an ancillary activity to any activity referred to in any of subparagraphs (i) to (iii) that the company is engaging in, computer equipment integral to the provision of information services related to the business of financial institutions or to the provision of financial services;

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: with the prior written approval of the Minister, develop, design, hold, manage, manufacture, sell or otherwise deal with data transmission systems, information sites, communication devices or information platforms or portals that are used

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: to provide information that is primarily financial or economic in nature,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: to provide information that relates to the business of a permitted entity, as defined in subsection 449(1), or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: for a prescribed purpose or in prescribed circumstances;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in Canada, engage in such activities referred to in paragraph (c) that the company was engaged in prior to June 1, 1992;

Paragraph.Label: (d.1)
Paragraph.Text: engage, under prescribed terms and conditions, if any are prescribed, in specialized business management or advisory services;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 410(3) of the Act is amended by striking out the word “and” at the end of paragraph (a), by adding the word “and” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the circumstances in which companies may be exempted from the requirement to obtain the approval of the Minister before carrying on a particular activity referred to in paragraph (1)(c) or (c.1).

Section.Text: Paragraphs 411(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: act as agent for any person in respect of the provision of any service that is provided by a financial institution, a permitted entity as defined in subsection 449(1) or a prescribed entity and may enter into an arrangement with any person in respect of the provision of that service; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: refer any person to any such financial institution or entity.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 414(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
A company shall not guarantee on behalf of any person the payment or repayment of any sum of money unless
Subsection 414(2) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:
Dans les cas où la personne visée au paragraphe (1) est une filiale de la société garante, celle-ci peut garantir une somme qui n’est pas fixe.

Section.Text: Section 417 of the Act is replaced by the following:
A company shall not engage in Canada in any personal property leasing activity in which a financial leasing entity, within the meaning of subsection 449(1), is not permitted to engage.
Section 419 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The directors of a company shall establish and the company shall adhere to policies regarding the creation of security interests in property of the company to secure obligations of the company and the acquisition by the company of beneficial interests in property that is subject to security interests.
The Superintendent may, by order, direct a company to amend its policies as specified in the order.
A company shall comply with an order made under subsection (2) within the time specified in the order.

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations and the Superintendent may make guidelines respecting the creation by a company of security interests in its property to secure obligations of the company and the acquisition by the company of beneficial interests in property that is subject to security interests.
Sections 419 and 419.1 do not apply in respect of a security interest created by a company to secure an obligation of the company to the Bank of Canada or the Canada Deposit Insurance Corporation.
Subsection 421(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Except with the approval of the Superintendent, a company may not be a general partner in a limited partnership or a partner in a general partnership.

Section.Text: Subsection 423(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A company is not bound to see to the execution of any trust to which any deposit made under the authority of this Act is subject, other than a trust of which the company is a trustee.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Payment when company has notice of trust
Subsection.Text: Subsection (6) applies regardless of whether the trust is express or arises by the operation of law, and it applies even when the company has notice of the trust if it acts on the order of or under the authority of the holder or holders of the account into which the deposit is made.

Section.Text: The heading “Interest and Charges” before section 426 of the Act is repealed.
The Act is amended by adding the following before section 426:
The following definitions apply in this section and in sections 431 to 434, 444.1 and 444.3.

Definition.Text: member company means a company that is a member institution as defined in section 2 of the Canada Deposit Insurance Corporation Act.
personal deposit account means a deposit account in the name of one or more natural persons that is kept by that person or those persons for a purpose other than that of carrying on business.
retail deposit account means a personal deposit account that is opened with a deposit of less than $150,000 or any greater amount that may be prescribed.

Section.Text: Subsection 427(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply in respect of an interest-bearing deposit account that is opened with a deposit in excess of $150,000 or any greater amount that may be prescribed.

Section.Text: Section 430 of the Act is repealed.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 431(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Disclosure required on opening a deposit account

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (2) to (4), a company shall not open a deposit account in the name of a customer unless, at or before the time the account is opened, the company provides in writing to the individual who requests the opening of the account
Subsections 431(2) to (5) of the Act are replaced by the following:

If a deposit account is not a personal deposit account and the amount of a charge applicable to the account cannot be established at or before the time the account is opened, the company shall, as soon as is practicable after the amount is established, provide the customer in whose name the account is kept with a notice in writing of the amount of the charge.

If a company has a deposit account in the name of a customer and the customer by telephone requests the opening of another deposit account in the name of the customer and the company has not complied with subsection (1) in respect of the opening of that other account, the company shall not open the account unless it provides the customer orally with any information prescribed at or before the time the account is opened.

If a company opens an account under subsection (3), it shall, not later than seven business days after the account is opened, provide to the customer in writing the agreement and information referred to in subsection (1).

A customer may, within 14 business days after a deposit account is opened under subsection (3), close the account without charge and in such case is entitled to a refund of any charges related to the operation of the account, other than interest charges, incurred while the account was open.

For the purposes of subsection (4), the Governor in Council may make regulations prescribing circumstances in which, and the time when, the agreement and information will be deemed to have been provided to the customer.

Section.Text: Section 434 of the Act is replaced by the following:
Sections 431 to 433 apply only in respect of charges applicable to deposit accounts with the company in Canada and services provided by the company in Canada.
Section 435 of the Act, as enacted by section 379 of An Act to amend certain laws relating to financial institutions, being chapter 15 of the Statutes of Canada, 1997, is replaced by the following:
For the purposes of this section and sections 435.1 to 442, cost of borrowing means, in respect of a loan made by a company,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the interest or discount applicable to the loan;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any amount charged in connection with the loan that is payable by the borrower to the company; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any charge prescribed to be included in the cost of borrowing.

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: For those purposes, however, cost of borrowing does not include any charge prescribed to be excluded from the cost of borrowing.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following before section 441:

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 441(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: establish procedures for dealing with complaints made by persons having requested or received products or services in Canada from the company;

Subsection.Text: If this section comes into force before paragraph 441(1)(a) of the Act, as enacted by section 382 of An Act to amend certain laws relating to financial institutions, being chapter 15 of the Statutes of Canada, 1997, comes into force, section 382 of that Act is repealed.
Subsection 441(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Procedures to be filed with Commissioner
Subsection.Text: A company shall file with the Commissioner a copy of its procedures established under paragraph (1)(a).

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 441:

Section.MarginalNote: Obligation to be member of complaints body
Section.Text: In any province, if there is no law of the province that makes a company subject to the jurisdiction of an organization that deals with complaints made by persons having requested or received products or services in the province from a company, the company shall be a member of an organization that is not controlled by it and that deals with those complaints that have not been resolved to the satisfaction of the persons under procedures established by companies under paragraph 441(1)(a).

Subsection.Text: Section 442 of the Act is replaced by the following:

A company shall, in the prescribed manner, provide a person requesting or receiving a product or service from it with prescribed information on how to contact the Agency if the person has a complaint about a deposit account, an arrangement referred to in subsection 438(3), a payment, credit or charge card, the disclosure of or manner of calculating the cost of borrowing in respect of a loan or about any other obligation of the company under a consumer provision.
The Commissioner shall prepare a report, to be included in the report referred to in section 34 of the Financial Consumer Agency of Canada Act, respecting

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: procedures for dealing with complaints established by companies pursuant to paragraph 441(1)(a); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the number and nature of complaints that have been brought to the attention of the Agency by persons who have requested or received a product or service from a company.

Subsection.Text: If this section comes into force before subsection 442(1) of the Act, as enacted by section 383 of An Act to amend certain laws relating to financial institutions, being chapter 15 of the Statutes of Canada, 1997, comes into force, section 383 of that Act is repealed.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 444:

Subsection.Text: Subject to regulations made under subsection (5), a member company with a branch in Canada at which it, through a natural person, opens retail deposit accounts and disburses cash to customers, shall give notice in accordance with those regulations before closing that branch or having it cease to carry on either of those activities.

After notice is given but before the branch is closed or ceases to carry on the activities, the Commissioner may, in prescribed situations, require the company to convene and hold a meeting between representatives of the company, representatives of the Agency and interested parties in the vicinity of the branch in order to exchange views about the closing or cessation of activities.
The Commissioner may establish rules for convening a meeting referred to in subsection (2) and for its conduct.
The Statutory Instruments Act does not apply in respect of rules established under subsection (3).
The Governor in Council may make regulations prescribing

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the manner and time, which may vary according to circumstances specified in the regulation, in which notice shall be given under subsection (1), to whom it shall be given and the information to be included;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: circumstances in which a member company is not required to give notice under subsection (1), circumstances in which the Commissioner may exempt a member company from the requirement to give notice under that subsection, and circumstances in which the Commissioner may vary the manner and time in which notice is required to be given under any regulation made under paragraph (a); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: circumstances in which a meeting may be convened under subsection (2).

Subsection.Text: A company with equity of $1 billion or more shall, in accordance with regulations made under subsection (4), annually publish a statement describing the contribution of the company and its prescribed affiliates to the Canadian economy and society.
A company shall, in the manner and at the time prescribed, file a copy of the statement with the Commissioner.
A company shall, in the manner and at the time prescribed, disclose the statement to its customers and to the public.
The Governor in Council may make regulations prescribing

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the name, contents and form of a statement referred to in subsection (1) and the time in which it must be prepared;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: affiliates of a company referred to in subsection (1);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the manner and time in which a statement must be filed under subsection (2); and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the manner and time in which a statement mentioned in subsection (3) is to be disclosed, respectively, to a company’s customers and to the public.

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may, subject to any other provisions of this Act relating to the disclosure of information, make regulations respecting the disclosure of information by companies or any prescribed class of companies, including regulations respecting

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the information that must be disclosed, including information relating to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: any product or service or prescribed class of products or services offered by them,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any of their policies, procedures or practices relating to the offer by them of any product or service or prescribed class of products or services,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: anything they are required to do or to refrain from doing under a consumer provision, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: any other matter that may affect their dealings with customers or the public;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the manner, place and time in which, and the persons to whom information is to be disclosed; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the content and form of any advertisement by companies or any prescribed class of companies relating to any matter referred to in paragraph (a).

Section.Text: Section 448 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (3) and (4), the following documents are binding on property belonging to a person and in the possession of a company, or on money owing to a person by reason of a deposit account in a company, only if the document or a notice of it is served at the branch of the company that has possession of the property or that is the branch of account in respect of the deposit account, as the case may be:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a writ or process originating a legal proceeding or issued in or pursuant to a legal proceeding;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an order or injunction made by a court;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an instrument purporting to assign, perfect or otherwise dispose of an interest in the property or the deposit account; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an enforcement notice in respect of a support order or support provision.

Subsection.Text: Any notification sent to a company with respect to a customer of the company, other than a document referred to in subsection (1) or (3), constitutes notice to the company and fixes the company with knowledge of its contents only if sent to and received at the branch of the company that is the branch of account of an account held in the name of that customer.
Subsections (1) and (2) do not apply in respect of an enforcement notice in respect of a support order or support provision if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the enforcement notice, accompanied by a written statement containing the information required by the regulations, is served at an office of a company designated in accordance with the regulations in respect of a province; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the order or provision can be enforced under the laws of that province.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (3) does not apply in respect of an enforcement notice in respect of a support order or support provision until the second business day following the day of service referred to in that subsection.
The Governor in Council may make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the designation by a company of a place, for the purpose of subsection (3), in any province for the service of enforcement notices in respect of support orders and support provisions;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the manner in which a company shall publicize the locations of designated offices of the company; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the information that must accompany enforcement notices in respect of support orders and support provisions.

Subsection.Text: The following definitions apply in this section.

Definition.Text: designated office means a place designated in accordance with regulations made for the purpose of subsection (3).

enforcement notice, in respect of a support order or support provision, means a garnishee summons or other instrument issued under the laws of a province for the enforcement of the support order or support provision.
support order means an order or judgment or interim order or judgment for family financial support.
support provision means a provision of an agreement relating to the payment of maintenance or family financial support.

Section.Text: Sections 449 to 471 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The following definitions apply in this Part.

Definition.Text: commercial loan means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any loan made or acquired by a company, other than

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a loan to a natural person in an amount of two hundred and fifty thousand dollars or less,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a loan to the Government of Canada, the government of a province, a municipality, or to any agency thereof, or to the government of a foreign country or any political subdivision thereof, or any agency thereof, or to a prescribed international agency,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a loan that is guaranteed by, or fully secured by securities issued by, a government, a municipality or an agency referred to in subparagraph (ii),

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a loan that is secured by a mortgage on real property, where

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the mortgage is on residential property and the amount of the loan, together with the amount then outstanding of any mortgage having an equal or prior claim against the property, does not exceed 75 % of the value of the property at the time the loan is made or acquired, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the mortgage is on real property other than residential property and

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: the amount of the loan, together with the amount then outstanding of any mortgage having an equal or prior claim against the property, does not exceed 75 % of the value of the property at the time the loan is made or acquired, and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: at the time the loan is made or acquired, the property provides an annual income sufficient to pay all annual expenses related to the property, including the payments owing under the mortgage and the mortgages having an equal or prior claim against the property,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: a loan that is secured by a mortgage on real property, where

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the mortgage is on residential property and

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: the amount of the loan, together with the amount then outstanding of any mortgage having an equal or prior claim against the property, exceeds 75 % of the value of the property at the time the loan is made or acquired, and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: repayment of the amount of the loan that exceeds 75 % of the value of the property is guaranteed or insured by a government agency or private insurer approved by the Superintendent,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the mortgage is on real property other than residential property and

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: the amount of the loan, together with the amount then outstanding of any mortgage having an equal or prior claim against the property, exceeds 75 % of the value of the property at the time the loan is made or acquired,

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: repayment of the amount of the loan that exceeds 75 % of the value of the property is guaranteed or insured by a government agency or private insurer approved by the Superintendent, and

Subclause.Label: (III)
Subclause.Text: at the time the loan is made or acquired, the property provides an annual income sufficient to pay all annual expenses related to the property, including the payments owing under the mortgage and the mortgages having an equal or prior claim against the property, or

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: the loan is one referred to in paragraph 418(2)(d),

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: a loan that

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: consists of a deposit made by the company with another financial institution,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: is fully secured by a deposit with any financial institution, including the company,

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: is fully secured by debt obligations guaranteed by any financial institution other than the company, or

Clause.Label: (D)
Clause.Text: is fully secured by a guarantee of a financial institution other than the company, or

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: a loan to an entity controlled by the company;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an investment in debt obligations, other than

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: debt obligations that are

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: guaranteed by any financial institution other than the company,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: fully secured by deposits with any financial institution, including the company, or

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: fully secured by debt obligations that are guaranteed by any financial institution other than the company,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: debt obligations issued by the Government of Canada, the government of a province, a municipality, or by any agency thereof, or by the government of a foreign country or any political subdivision thereof, or by any agency thereof, or by a prescribed international agency,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: debt obligations that are guaranteed by, or fully secured by securities issued by, a government, a municipality or an agency referred to in subparagraph (ii),

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: debt obligations that are widely distributed, as that expression is defined by the regulations, or

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: debt obligations of an entity controlled by the company; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an investment in shares of a body corporate or ownership interests in an unincorporated entity, other than

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: shares or ownership interests that are widely distributed, as that expression is defined by the regulations,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: shares or ownership interests of an entity controlled by the company, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: participating shares.

Definition.Text: factoring entity means a factoring entity as defined in the regulations.
finance entity means a finance entity as defined in the regulations.
financial leasing entity means an entity

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the activities of which are limited to the financial leasing of personal property and such related activities as are prescribed and whose activities conform to such restrictions and limitations thereon as are prescribed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that, in conducting the activities referred to in paragraph (a) in Canada, does not

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: direct its customers or potential customers to particular dealers in the leased property or the property to be leased,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: enter into lease agreements with persons in respect of any motor vehicle having a gross vehicle weight, as that expression is defined by the regulations, of less than twenty-one tonnes, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: enter into lease agreements with natural persons in respect of personal household property, as that expression is defined by the regulations.

Definition.Text: loan includes an acceptance, endorsement or other guarantee, a deposit, a financial lease, a conditional sales contract, a repurchase agreement and any other similar arrangement for obtaining funds or credit but does not include investments in securities.
motor vehicle means a motorized vehicle designed to be used primarily on a public highway for the transportation of persons or things, but does not include

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a fire-engine, bus, ambulance or utility truck; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any other special purpose motorized vehicle that contains significant special features that make it suitable for a specific purpose.

Definition.Text: mutual fund distribution entity means an entity whose principal activity is acting as a selling agent of units, shares or other interests in a mutual fund and acting as a collecting agent in the collection of payments for any such interests if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the proceeds of the sales of any such interests, less any sales commissions and service fees, are paid to the mutual fund; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the existence of a sales commission and service fee in respect of the sale of any such interest is disclosed to the purchaser of the interest before the purchase of the interest.

Definition.Text: mutual fund entity means an entity

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: whose activities are limited to the investing of the funds of the entity so as to provide investment diversification and professional investment management to the holders of its securities; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: whose securities entitle their holders to receive, on demand, or within a specified period after demand, an amount computed by reference to the value of a proportionate interest in the whole or in a part of its net assets, including a separate fund or trust account of the entity.

Definition.Text: participating share means a share of a body corporate that carries the right to participate in the earnings of the body corporate to an unlimited degree and to participate in a distribution of the remaining property of the body corporate on dissolution.
permitted entity means an entity in which a company is permitted to acquire a substantial investment under section 453.
prescribed subsidiary means a subsidiary that is one of a prescribed class of subsidiaries.
specialized financing entity means a specialized financing entity as defined in the regulations.

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of this Part, a member of a company’s group is any of the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an entity referred to in any of paragraphs 453(1)(a) to (f) that controls the company;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a subsidiary of the company or of an entity referred to in any of paragraphs 453(1)(a) to (f) that controls the company;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an entity in which the company, or an entity referred to in any of paragraphs 453(1)(a) to (f) that controls the company, has a substantial investment; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a prescribed entity in relation to the company.

Subsection.Text: This Part does not apply in respect of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: money or other assets held in trust by a company, other than guaranteed trust money and assets held in respect thereof;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the holding of a security interest in real property, unless the security interest is prescribed pursuant to paragraph 467(a) to be an interest in real property; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the holding of a security interest in securities of an entity.

Section.Text: The directors of a company shall establish and the company shall adhere to investment and lending policies, standards and procedures that a reasonable and prudent person would apply in respect of a portfolio of investments and loans to avoid undue risk of loss and obtain a reasonable return.

Section.MarginalNote: Restriction on control and substantial investments

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (2) to (4), no company shall acquire control of, or hold, acquire or increase a substantial investment in, any entity other than a permitted entity.
A company may, subject to Part XI, acquire control of, or acquire or increase a substantial investment in, an entity other than a permitted entity by way of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an acquisition of control of an entity referred to in any of paragraphs 453(1)(a) to (j), or of a prescribed entity, that controls or has a substantial investment in the entity; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an acquisition of shares or ownership interests in the entity by

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an entity referred to in any of paragraphs 453(1)(a) to (j), or a prescribed entity, that is controlled by the company, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an entity controlled by an entity referred to in any of paragraphs 453(1)(a) to (j), or a prescribed entity, that is controlled by the company.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Exception: temporary investments, realizations and loan workouts
Subsection.Text: A company may, subject to Part XI, acquire control of, or acquire or increase a substantial investment in, an entity by way of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a temporary investment permitted by section 456;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an acquisition of shares of a body corporate or of ownership interests in an unincorporated entity permitted by section 457; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a realization of security permitted by section 458.

Subsection.Text: A company may, subject to Part XI, acquire control of, or hold, acquire or increase a substantial investment in, an entity other than a permitted entity if it does so in accordance with regulations made under paragraph 452(d) concerning specialized financing.

A company is deemed not to contravene subsection (1) if the company acquires control of, or acquires or increases a substantial investment in, an entity solely as the result of an event not within the control of the company.

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the determination of the amount or value of loans, investments and interests for the purposes of this Part;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the loans and investments, and the maximum aggregate amount of all loans and investments, that may be made or acquired by a company and its prescribed subsidiaries to or in a person and any persons connected with that person;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: specifying the classes of persons who are connected with any person for the purposes of paragraph (b); and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: concerning specialized financing for the purposes of subsection 451(4).

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (6) to (8) and Part XI, a company may acquire control of, or acquire or increase a substantial investment in

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a company;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a bank;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a bank holding company;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an association to which the Cooperative Credit Associations Act applies;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: an insurance company or a fraternal benefit society incorporated or formed under the Insurance Companies Act;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: an insurance holding company;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: a trust, loan or insurance corporation incorporated or formed by or under an Act of the legislature of a province;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: a cooperative credit society incorporated or formed, and regulated, by or under an Act of the legislature of a province;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: an entity that is incorporated or formed by or under an Act of Parliament or of the legislature of a province and that is primarily engaged in dealing in securities; or

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: an entity that is incorporated or formed, and regulated, otherwise than by or under an Act of Parliament or of the legislature of a province and that is primarily engaged outside Canada in a business that, if carried on in Canada, would be the business of banking, the business of a cooperative credit society, the business of insurance, the business of providing fiduciary services or the business of dealing in securities.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (3) to (6) and Part XI, a company may acquire control of, or acquire or increase a substantial investment in, an entity, other than an entity referred to in any of paragraphs (1)(a) to (j), whose business is limited to one or more of the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: engaging in any financial service activity or in any other activity that a company is permitted to engage in under any of paragraphs 409(2)(b) to (d) or section 410 or 411;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: acquiring or holding shares of, or ownership interests in, entities in which a company is permitted under this Part to hold or acquire;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: engaging in the provision of any services exclusively to any or all of the following, so long as the entity is providing those services to the company or any member of the company’s group:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the company,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any member of the company’s group,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: any entity that is primarily engaged in the business of providing financial services,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: any permitted entity in which an entity referred to in subparagraph (iii) has a substantial investment, or

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: any prescribed person, if it is doing so under prescribed terms and conditions, if any are prescribed;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: engaging in any activity that a company is permitted to engage in, other than an activity referred to in paragraph (a) or (e), that relates to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the promotion, sale, delivery or distribution of a financial product or financial service that is provided by the company or any member of the company’s group, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if a significant portion of the business of the entity involves an activity referred to in subparagraph (i), the promotion, sale, delivery or distribution of a financial product or financial service that is provided by any other entity that is primarily engaged in the business of providing financial services;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: engaging in the activities referred to in the definition mutual fund entity or mutual fund distribution entity in subsection 449(1); and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: engaging in prescribed activities, under prescribed terms and conditions, if any are prescribed.

Subsection.Text: A company may not acquire control of, or acquire or increase a substantial investment in, an entity whose business includes any activity referred to in any of paragraphs (2)(a) to (e) if the entity engages in the business of accepting deposit liabilities or if the activities of the entity include

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: activities that a company is not permitted to engage in under any of sections 417 and 418;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: dealing in securities, except as may be permitted under paragraph (2)(e) or as may be permitted to a company under paragraph 409(2)(c);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: acting as an executor, administrator or official guardian or as a guardian, tutor, curator, judicial adviser or committee of a mentally incompetent person;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: being a trustee for a trust;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: activities that a company is not permitted to engage in under any regulation made under section 416 if the entity engages in the activities of a finance entity or of any other entity as may be prescribed;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: acquiring control of or acquiring or holding a substantial investment in another entity unless

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of an entity that is controlled by the company, the company itself would be permitted under this Part to acquire a substantial investment in the other entity, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of an entity that is not controlled by the company, the company itself would be permitted to acquire a substantial investment in the other entity under subsection (1) or (2) or 451(2), paragraph 451(3)(b) or (c) or subsection 451(4); or

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: any prescribed activity.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (8) and the regulations, a company may not acquire control of, or acquire or increase a substantial investment in,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an entity referred to in any of paragraphs (1)(a) to (j), unless

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the company controls, within the meaning of paragraph 3(1)(d), the entity, or would thereby acquire control, within the meaning of that paragraph, of the entity, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the company is permitted by regulations made under paragraph 459(a) to acquire or increase the substantial investment;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an entity whose business includes one or more of the activities referred to in paragraph (2)(a) and that engages, as part of its business, in any financial intermediary activity that exposes the entity to material market or credit risk, including a finance entity, a factoring entity and a financial leasing entity, unless

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the company controls, within the meaning of paragraph 3(1)(d), the entity, or would thereby acquire control, within the meaning of that paragraph, of the entity, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the company is permitted by regulations made under paragraph 459(a) to acquire or increase the substantial investment; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an entity whose business includes an activity referred to in paragraph (2)(b), including a specialized financing entity, unless

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the company controls, within the meaning of paragraph 3(1)(d), the entity, or would thereby acquire control, within the meaning of that paragraph, of the entity,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the company is permitted by regulations made under paragraph 459(a) to acquire or increase the substantial investment, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: subject to prescribed terms and conditions, if any are prescribed, the activities of the entity do not include the acquisition or holding of control of, or the acquisition or holding of shares or other ownership interests in, an entity referred to in paragraph (a) or (b) or an entity that is not a permitted entity.

Subsection.Text: Subject to the regulations, a company may not, without the prior written approval of the Minister,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: acquire control of an entity referred to in paragraphs (1)(g) to (i) from a person who is not a member of the company’s group;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: acquire control of an entity referred to in paragraph (1)(j) or (4)(b), other than an entity whose activities are limited to the activities of one or more of the following entities, if the control is acquired from an entity referred to in any of paragraphs (1)(a) to (f) that is not a member of the company’s group:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a factoring entity, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a financial leasing entity;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: acquire control of, or acquire or increase a substantial investment in, an entity whose business includes one or more of the activities referred to in paragraph (2)(d);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: acquire control of, or acquire or increase a substantial investment in, an entity that engages in an activity described in paragraph 410(1)(c) or (c.1); or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: acquire control of, or acquire or increase a substantial investment in, an entity engaging in an activity prescribed for the purposes of paragraph (2)(f).

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (7) and the regulations, a company may not acquire control of, or acquire or increase a substantial investment in, an entity referred to in any of paragraphs (1)(g) to (j) and (4)(b) and (c) unless the company obtains the approval of the Superintendent.
Subsection (6) does not apply in respect of a particular transaction if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the company is acquiring control of an entity whose business includes an activity referred to in paragraph (2)(b), other than a specialized financing entity;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the company is acquiring control of an entity whose activities are limited to the activities of a factoring entity or a financial leasing entity; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister has approved the transaction under subsection (5) or is deemed to have approved it under subsection 454(1).

Subsection.Text: A company need not control an entity referred to in paragraph (1)(j), or an entity that is incorporated or formed otherwise than by or under an Act of Parliament or of the legislature of a province, if the laws or customary business practices of the country under the laws of which the entity was incorporated or formed do not permit the company to control the entity.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Prohibition on giving up control in fact
Subsection.Text: A company that, under subsection (4), controls an entity may not, without the prior written approval of the Minister, give up control, within the meaning of paragraph 3(1)(d), of the entity while it continues to control the entity.

A company that, under subsection (4), controls an entity may, with the prior written approval of the Superintendent, give up control of the entity while keeping a substantial investment in the entity if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the company is permitted to do so by regulations made under paragraph 459(c); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the entity meets the conditions referred to in subparagraph (4)(c)(iii).

Subsection.Text: If a company controls, within the meaning of paragraph 3(1)(a), (b) or (c), an entity, subsections (5) and (6) do not apply in respect of any subsequent increases by the company of its substantial investment in the entity so long as the company continues to control the entity.

If a company obtains the approval of the Minister under subsection 453(5) to acquire control of, or to acquire or increase a substantial investment in, an entity and, through that acquisition or increase, the company indirectly acquires control of, or acquires or increases a substantial investment in, another entity that would require the approval of the Minister under subsection 453(5) or the Superintendent under subsection 453(6) and that indirect acquisition or increase is disclosed to the Minister in writing before the approval is obtained, the company is deemed to have obtained the approval of the Minister or the Superintendent for that indirect acquisition or increase.

If a company obtains the approval of the Superintendent under subsection 453(6) to acquire control of, or to acquire or increase a substantial investment in, an entity and, through that acquisition or increase the company indirectly acquires control of, or acquires or increases a substantial investment in, another entity that would require the approval of the Superintendent under that subsection and that indirect acquisition or increase is disclosed to the Superintendent in writing before the approval is obtained, the company is deemed to have obtained the approval of the Superintendent for that indirect acquisition or increase.

If a company controls a permitted entity, other than an entity referred to in any of paragraphs 453(1)(a) to (f), the company shall provide the Superintendent with any undertakings that the Superintendent may require regarding

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the activities of the entity; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: access to information about the entity.

Subsection.Text: If a company acquires control of an entity referred to in any of paragraphs 453(1)(g) to (j), the company shall provide the Superintendent with any undertakings concerning the entity that the Superintendent may require.

The Superintendent may enter into an agreement with the appropriate official or public body responsible for the supervision of an entity referred to in any of paragraphs 453(1)(g) to (j) in each province or in any other jurisdiction concerning any matters referred to in paragraphs (1)(a) and (b) or any other matter the Superintendent considers appropriate.

Despite any other provision of this Part, a company shall not control a permitted entity, other than an entity referred to in any of paragraphs 453(1)(a) to (f), unless, in the course of the acquisition of control or within a reasonable time after the control is acquired, the company obtains from the permitted entity an undertaking to provide the Superintendent with reasonable access to the records of the permitted entity.

Subject to subsection (4), a company may, by way of a temporary investment, acquire control of, or acquire or increase a substantial investment in, an entity but, within two years, or any other period that may be specified or approved by the Superintendent, after acquiring control or after acquiring or increasing the substantial investment, as the case may be, it shall do all things necessary to ensure that it no longer controls the entity or has a substantial investment in the entity.
